text:
There once was a teenage boy named Tim. Tim was an exceptionally good athlete who could compete at high school level levels. But something peculiar happened each time he played football—it felt more like practice than playing. He felt like he didn't really have control over the ball.

One day, while talking to his friend Dave about it, Dave suggested they might be practicing something called "mindfulness". Tim hadn't heard of it but was intrigued.

He started practicing mindfulness while playing. Every catch felt like he was flying; every snap felt precise. He felt like he could hear his own breath in the quiet moments between plays.

The seasons passed, and football seasons came and went. Tim felt like he was truly playing now. There were no practice plays—it was just Tim, playing with the grace and confidence of someone fully present.

Before the big game, Tim attended a mindfulness workshop. All the young athletes there were there to find the same thing he had discovered: a kind of silent strength that wasn't
